prompt: Add Observe SDK instrumentation to this LlamaIndex agent file: examples/llama-index-example.py
expectations: Observe.init() is called at module level with an app_name and api_endpoint using os.getenv
from ioa_observe.sdk import Observe is present in imports
from ioa_observe.sdk.decorators import tool, agent, graph (or similar combination) is present
from ioa_observe.sdk.tracing import session_start is present
@tool decorator is applied to the multiply function
@tool decorator is applied to the add function
@agent decorator is applied to MathAgentWorkflow class
@graph decorator is applied to MathAgentWorkflow class (stacked above @agent)
session_start() is called in the main() function before running the workflow
The file remains functionally equivalent - all original logic is preserved

prompt: Instrument examples/llama-index-example.py with the ioa observe sdk so I can trace my agent
expectations: Observe.init() is called with app_name parameter
Observe SDK imports are added (ioa_observe.sdk)
@tool decorator wraps the multiply function
@tool decorator wraps the add function
MathAgentWorkflow class has both @graph and @agent decorators applied
session_start() call appears in main() before the workflow runs
OTLP_HTTP_ENDPOINT environment variable is used in Observe.init()
No existing code is removed or broken - only additions are made

prompt: Add observability to examples/llama-index-example.py using the observe SDK in this repo
expectations: Observe.init() is added with a meaningful app_name string
ioa_observe decorators are imported
Both tool functions (multiply, add) have @tool decorator
MathAgentWorkflow has @agent decorator
MathAgentWorkflow has @graph decorator stacked above @agent
session_start() is added to the main async function
The modified file is shown to the user or the edits are applied
A summary of what was changed is provided to the user

prompt: Add Observe SDK instrumentation to this LangGraph agent: evals/fixtures/langgraph-uninstrumented.py
expectations: Observe.init() is called at module level with an app_name and api_endpoint using os.getenv
from ioa_observe.sdk import Observe is present in imports
from ioa_observe.sdk.decorators import agent, graph and tool (or observe_tool alias) are imported
from ioa_observe.sdk.tracing import session_start is present
@observe_tool (or @tool from ioa_observe) decorator is applied to get_exchange_rate, stacked below @langchain_tool
@observe_tool (or @tool from ioa_observe) decorator is applied to convert_currency, stacked below @langchain_tool
@agent decorator is applied to currency_agent_node function
@graph decorator is applied to build_graph function
session_start() is called in main() before the agent invocation loop
The file remains functionally equivalent - all original logic is preserved

prompt: Instrument evals/fixtures/langgraph-uninstrumented.py with the ioa observe sdk so I can trace my LangGraph agent
expectations: Observe.init() is called with a meaningful app_name and OTLP_HTTP_ENDPOINT from os.getenv

prompt: Make my LangGraph currency converter agent observable using the observe SDK: evals/fixtures/langgraph-uninstrumented.py
expectations: Observe.init() is added after load_dotenv()
Observe SDK imports are present
Both tool functions are wrapped with the observe @tool decorator
The agent node function currency_agent_node is decorated with @agent
The graph builder function build_graph is decorated with @graph
session_start() appears in main() before the while loop or before agent invocation
Observe.init() uses a descriptive app_name string relevant to the service (not a placeholder)
The file remains functionally equivalent
